title: Total Nucleic Acids Extraction from Soil
author: Roey Angel
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bwxcpfiw
materials: Prepare the following solutions for TNA extraction Use clean and preferably baked glassware (make sure all non-glass components can withstand the high temperatures).
45 mL, 120 mM PB pH 8.0 Dissolve in PB. Autoclave. Store at Room temperature Potassium phosphate dibasic trihydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P9666 (CAS: 16788-57-
Potassium phosphate monobasic Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P9791 (CAS: 7778-77-
Aluminium ammonium sulfate dodecahydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #1010310500 (CAS: 7784-26-1)

undetermined, RNase-free water Dissolve the salts in RNase-free water and fill up to 200 ml. Autoclave. Store at Room temperature Trizma® base Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #93362 (CAS: 77-86-

Sodium chloride for molecular biology Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #S3014 Hexadecyltrimethylammonium bromide Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #H6269 Sodium dodecyl sulfate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #3771
Molecular-grade ethanol solution (75%) 75 mL, Absolute ethanol 25 mL, RNase-free water Store at -20 °C Ethanol, Absolute, Molecular Biology Grade Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #BP2818500

Low-EDTA TE buffer undetermined, Tris-HCl 1 M, pH 8.0 (Trizma) undetermined, EDTA 0.5 M, pH 8.0 undetermined, RNase-free water Prepare in a laminar-flow hood (to protect stocks), filter sterilise (0.2 µm) and autoclave. Store at Room temperature UltraPure™ 1M Tris-HCI pH 8.0 Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #15568025 UltraPure™ 0.5M EDTA pH 8.0 Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #15575020

steps.title: undetermined, RNase-free water Dissolve the salts...
steps.instruction: undetermined, RNase-free water Dissolve the salts in RNase-free water and fill up to 500 ml. Autoclave. Phosphate buffer ( 120 millimolar (mM) , pH 5.8 ) undetermined, K2HPO4•3 H2O (M.W. 136.

steps.title: undetermined, RNase-free water Dissolve the salts...
steps.instruction: undetermined, RNase-free water Dissolve the salts in RNase-free water and fill up to 500 ml. Autoclave. Phosphate buffer + AlNH 4 (120 mM PB pH 8.0 + 0.1M AlNH 4 (SO 4 ) 2 • 12H 2 O) 2.04 g, AlNH4(SO4)2 · 12H2O (M.W. 453.

steps.title: Nuclease-free autoclaved DEPC-treated water Carl Roth Catalog #T143
steps.instruction: Nuclease-free autoclaved DEPC-treated water Carl Roth Catalog #T143.1 (CAS: 7732-18-

steps.title: undetermined, RNase-free water Dissolve the salts...
steps.instruction: undetermined, RNase-free water Dissolve the salts in RNase-free water and fill up to 200 ml. Autoclave. TNS undetermined, TRIZMA (M.W 121.

steps.title: PEG precipitation solution (30%) undetermined, PEG (M
steps.instruction: PEG precipitation solution (30%) undetermined, PEG (M.W 7000-9000) undetermined, NaCl undetermined, RNase-free water Add ingredients to a graduated Duran bottle. Add water to fill up to 200 ml, shake vigorously by hand, autoclave and mix well while hot (solution turns milky when hot, but then turns clear when cooled to room temperature). Store at Room temperature Polyethylene glycol 8000 Carl Roth Catalog #0263.1

steps.title: Weigh 0
steps.instruction: Weigh 0.4 g, soil (0.2-0.7 g) into a Lysing Matrix E tube, or a 2-ml screw-cap tube filled with 0.7 g (one full PCR tube) of 0.1 mm glass beads and place the tube On ice . Lysing Matrix E 2 mL tube MP Biomedicals Catalog #116914050-CF

steps.title: or water-saturated phenol (if using PB pH 5
steps.instruction: or water-saturated phenol (if using PB pH 5.8). Alternatively, add 675 µL of PB, 225 µL of TNC or TNS, if not using phenol (see Guidelines and Warnings on which combination of solutions to choose.) ROTI® Phenol Carl Roth Catalog #0038.1 (CAS: 108-95-

steps.title: Lysing Matrix E tubes with soil...
steps.instruction: Lysing Matrix E tubes with soil and reagents prepared for the 1st bead beating round.

steps.title: Immediately place the tube in a...
steps.instruction: Immediately place the tube in a sample homogeniser and process for 00:00:30 at 6.5 m s -1 . We recommend using the FastPrep-24™ sample homogenizer with the CoolPrep™ adapter for 24 x 2 ml On ice, (dry ice) . If using the CoolPrep™ adapter make sure all slots are filled with either sample or empty tubes. The bottom part of a CoolPrep FastPrep adaptor filled with the recommended amount of dry ice. FastPrep-24™ 5G bead beating grinder and lysis system (Sample homogenizer, MP Biomedicals, 116005500, https://www.mpbio.com/au/fastprep-24-5g-instrument ) CoolPrep™ adapter for 24 x 2 mL tube holder on FastPrep-24 (Sample homogeniser adapter, MP Biomedicals, 116002528, https://www.mpbio.com/au/catalogsearch/result/?q=coolprep ) Dry Ice Contributed by users FastPrep-24™ 5G set up. The green-capped tubes are empty and are used to seal the CoolPrep adapter. The samples right after bead beating.

steps.title: Chill On ice for 00:00:10 (...
steps.instruction: Chill On ice for 00:00:10 ( not necessary if using the CoolPrep™ adapter ).

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14000 rpm, 15 °C,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14000 rpm, 15 °C, 3 minutes (centrifugation at RT is also possible). The samples after centrifugation.

steps.title: Transfer the entire liquid (aqueous and...
steps.instruction: Transfer the entire liquid (aqueous and organic phases) to a fresh 2 ml tube by decanting or pipetting. tubes. Your supernatant has two phases. An aqueous upper phase that should be transparent/translucent and a phenol phase that should be opaque. Make sure to pour both into the fresh 2 ml tube. Tubes set up for 8 samples: A) Lysing Matrix E in a green stand (1 tube per sample). B) 2 ml tubes for collecting the supernatant after processing in a yellow stand (2 tubes per sample). The collected supernatant at this stage.

steps.title: Repeat Steps 3-7 two more times...
steps.instruction: Repeat Steps 3-7 two more times using the same Lysing Matrix E tube. Be careful not to overfill the tube as this might cause phenol leakage during the sample homogenising process. If there is not enough space in the tube for all the reagents, decrease the phenol volume (down to 200 μl). For the second homogenising repetition use a fresh 2 ml tube to collect the supernatant in Step 7 , while for the third repetition divide the supernatant between the two tubes from the previous repetitions to achieve equal volumes. go to step #3

steps.title: Add 800 µL, phenol/chloroform/isoamyl alcohol 25:24:1...
steps.instruction: Add 800 µL, phenol/chloroform/isoamyl alcohol 25:24:1 (or 1 volume) to each of the tubes containing the supernatant, to a maximum of 2 ml total volume in each tube. ROTI® Phenol/ Chloroform/ Isoamyl alcohol Carl Roth Catalog #A156.1 (CAS: 136112-00-0)

steps.title: Mix the two phases , by...
steps.instruction: Mix the two phases , by hand or using a vortex. Centrifuge 14000 rpm, 15 °C, 3 minutes . Following centrifugation, the liquid in each tube should separate into two phases.

steps.title: Using a 1-ml pipette tip, carefully...
steps.instruction: Using a 1-ml pipette tip, carefully transfer the aqueous phase (the upper phase) from each tube to two fresh 2 ml tubes. Be careful not to touch or pipette the interphase or the organic phase (the lower phase) If, by mistake, some of the interphase or organic phase were pipetted, simply return the liquid to the original tube and centrifuge it again.

steps.title: Add undetermined, chloroform/isoamyl alcohol 24:1 (or...
steps.instruction: Add undetermined, chloroform/isoamyl alcohol 24:1 (or 1 volume) to each tube. ROTI® C/I Carl Roth Catalog #X984.1

steps.title: Mix the phases vigorously by hand or using a vortex
steps.instruction: Mix the phases vigorously by hand or using a vortex. Centrifuge 14000 rpm, 15 °C, 3 minutes . Following centrifugation, the liquid in each tube should separate into two phases.

steps.title: Carefully transfer the supernatant from each...
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer the supernatant from each tube to a fresh 2 ml low-binding microcentrifuge tubes. At this point, you should have two low-binding tubes per sample. If, by mistake, some of the interphase or organic phase were pipetted, simply return the liquid to the original tube and centrifuge it again. DNA LoBind Tubes (Microcentrifuge tubes, Eppendorf, 0030108051, https://online-shop.eppendorf.com/OC-en/Laboratory-Consumables-44512/Tubes-44515/DNA-LoBind-Tubes-PF-56252.html )

steps.title: Add to each tube 2 µL,...
steps.instruction: Add to each tube 2 µL, RNA-grade glycogen and 1 mL, PEG Precipitation Solution (or twice the extract’s volume). Glycogen RNA grade Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #R0551 (CAS: 9005-79-2)

steps.title: Centrifuge 14000 rpm, 4 °C, 1 hour
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 14000 rpm, 4 °C, 1 hour . A pellet should be visible at the bottom of the LoBind tube after centrifugation. The pellet should be white/opaque in colour. The size of the pellet will depend on the TNA content in a sample, but also on the amount of co-extracted contaminants.

steps.title: Decant the supernatant, briefly centrifuge or...
steps.instruction: Decant the supernatant, briefly centrifuge or spin-down again to collect the drops and using a pipette remove as much as possible from the remaining precipitation solution. Be careful not to disturb the pellet.

steps.title: Add 1 mL, ice-cold 75% EtOH...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL, ice-cold 75% EtOH , invert the tube several times. Centrifuge at 14000 rpm, 4 °C, 20 minutes .

steps.title: Remove the supernatant using a pipette,...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant using a pipette, shortly centrifuge again to collect the drops and using a pipette remove as much as possible from the remaining ethanol. Be careful not to disturb the pellet.

steps.title: Leave the tubes open at Room...
steps.instruction: Leave the tubes open at Room temperature for approximately 00:05:00 (preferably under a flame, inside a laminar-flow hood, or inverted on a piece of kitchen paper, to prevent contamination) to evaporate the remaining ethanol. Alternatively, the pellets can be dried under a filtered stream of N 2 , or in a speed-vac. The pellets might not be completely dry at this point, but the remaining liquid should be pure water.

steps.title: Resuspend each pellet in 50 µL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend each pellet in 50 µL to 100 µL, Low-EDTA TE Buffer and combine both subsamples into one of the non-stick tubes.

steps.title: Remove co-extracted humic substances using OneStep™...
steps.instruction: Remove co-extracted humic substances using OneStep™ PCR Inhibitor Removal Kit This step is optional but very recommended. OneStep PCR Inhibitor Removal Kit Zymo Research Catalog #D6030

steps.title: Resuspend the resin in the column...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the resin in the column by brief vortexing (if the column is dry, add 100 μl of RNAse-free water prior to vortexing.)

steps.title: Loosen the cap by a quarter...
steps.instruction: Loosen the cap by a quarter twist, place the column in a collection tube and centrifuge 8000 x g, Room temperature, 3 minutes

steps.title: Place the column in a fresh 1
steps.instruction: Place the column in a fresh 1.5 ml tube, pipette the entire TNA extract (up to 200 μl) on top of the resin. Be careful not to disturb the resin with the tip

steps.title: Centrifuge 8000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 8000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute

steps.title: Discard the column and retain the extract in the tube
steps.instruction: Discard the column and retain the extract in the tube.

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: load 5 µL to 10 µL of the total NA extract on an agarose gel to evaluate its quantity and quality using electrophoresis.

steps.title: Quantify total NA extract using RiboGreen™...
steps.instruction: Quantify total NA extract using RiboGreen™ and PicoGreen™ (see Guidelines and Warnings ) or only DNA using PicoGreen™. Qant-iT™ PicoGreen ® dsDNA Quantification by Roey Angel

steps.title: The extract can be used directly...
steps.instruction: The extract can be used directly as a DNA template for downstream applications. We recommend aliquoting 10 µL to 100 µL as a work-template to be stored at -20 °C , in order to minimise freeze-thaw cycles. Genomic DNA Clean & Concentrator-10 Zymo Research Catalog #D4011

steps.title: Depending on quality and quantity, aliquot...
steps.instruction: Depending on quality and quantity, aliquot 10 µL to 100 µL for RNA purification. Proceed to Purification of RNA from a DNA/RNA Extract. Purification of RNA from a DNA/RNA Extract by Roey Angel

steps.title: If the removal of RNA is...
steps.instruction: If the removal of RNA is necessary, follow up with RNaseH treatment and purification using e.g. Genomic DNA Clean & Concentrator. Genomic DNA Clean & Concentrator-10 Zymo Research Catalog #D4011
